description_long: This object is a classic regulatory stop sign, featuring an octagonal red face with a white border and the word 'STOP' prominently displayed in bold, uppercase white letters. The sign face shows signs of wear, with visible scratches and a slightly weathered texture, suggesting outdoor use. The sign is mounted on a long, slender, black metal pole, which is typical for installation at roadways or intersections. The pole appears sturdy and is likely made of steel or a similar metal, providing durability and resistance to environmental elements. The reverse side of the sign is plain and gray, with visible mounting hardware connecting the sign face to the pole. The overall construction is utilitarian, designed for high visibility and longevity in urban environments.
description: A weathered red octagonal stop sign with white 'STOP' text, mounted on a black metal pole. The sign shows scratches and is built for outdoor urban use.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the gray back of the octagonal sign and the mounting hardware attaching it to the black pole.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the sign face and the length of the supporting pole.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the red octagonal sign with bold white 'STOP' text and a white border.
description_view_3: Angled perspective showing both the red sign face and the length of the black pole.
